rules.name: Validity of Arbitration Agreements
rules.text: Arbitration agreements are valid, irrevocable, and enforceable under the Federal Arbitration Act.

elements.name: Binding Agreement
elements.text: A written arbitration agreement is enforceable unless legal grounds exist for revocation of any contract.

rules.name: Interstate Commerce Requirement
rules.text: The FAA applies when the underlying transaction involves interstate commerce.

elements.name: Commerce Nexus
elements.text: The dispute must involve goods, services, or conduct affecting interstate commerce.

rules.name: Subpoena Authority
rules.text: The arbitrator may issue subpoenas for witness testimony and document production.

elements.name: Prima Facie Relevance
elements.text: The requesting party must show minimal evidence that the subpoenaed material is relevant and material.

elements.name: Written Testimony
elements.text: The arbitrator may require written testimony or documents prior to the close of the written hearing.

exceptions.name: beyond_reasonable_doubt
exceptions.text: Criminal burdens of proof do not apply to FAA subpoenas.

rules.name: Award Confirmation
rules.text: A party may apply to a court for confirmation of the arbitration award.

elements.name: Mandatory Confirmation
elements.text: The court must confirm the award unless grounds exist under FAA §10 or §11.

rules.name: Award Vacatur
rules.text: An arbitration award may be vacated only under the limited grounds specified in FAA §10.

elements.name: Corruption or Fraud
elements.text: The award may be vacated if procured by corruption, fraud, or undue means.

elements.name: Misconduct
elements.text: Vacatur may occur if the arbitrator is guilty of misconduct or refuses to hear material evidence.

elements.name: Exceeding Powers
elements.text: Vacatur may occur if the arbitrator exceeds their powers.

rules.name: Award Modification
rules.text: A court may modify or correct an arbitration award under FAA §11.

elements.name: Evident Material Miscalculation
elements.text: Awards may be corrected for evident material miscalculations or mistakes.

elements.name: Form Corrections
elements.text: Awards may be corrected for imperfections in form not affecting the merits.

rules.name: FAA Preemption
rules.text: The FAA preempts state laws that conflict with its enforcement of arbitration agreements.

elements.name: Conflict Preemption
elements.text: State rules that undermine arbitration agreements are preempted.

definitions.text: A written agreement to submit disputes to arbitration.
A minimal showing of relevance required for subpoena issuance.
Judicial cancellation of an arbitration award under FAA §10.
Judicial correction of an arbitration award under FAA §11.
